surface010_blocker_definition.root_cause: Two direct triggers both use in-memory data structures that require runtime initialization before use

direct_triggers.mechanism: ContextVar[CurrentUser | None] — per-request user state stored in memory
direct_triggers.write_operation: set_current_user(user) stores user in ContextVar memory
direct_triggers.isolation_mechanism: asyncio task-local — each FastAPI request is a separate task

direct_triggers.mechanism: init_engine() performs startup DB writes (CREATE DATABASE, WAL mode, schema create_all)
write_operations: CREATE DATABASE — postgres only, run once at startup
Base.metadata.create_all — creates all tables at startup
direct_triggers.memory_backend_fallback: backend=memory → init_engine is no-op, get_session_factory returns None

surface010_blocker_definition.why_blocked: Both triggers involve in-memory state that could leak or cause undefined behavior if not properly initialized before first use

DT-001_user_context.mechanism: ContextVar[CurrentUser | None] with Token-based reset pattern

async_task_isolation.mechanism: ContextVar is task-local under asyncio, not thread-local
async_task_isolation.guarantee: Each asyncio.create_task() gets its own copy of the context
async_task_isolation.inheritance: Parent task's context is inherited by child tasks (intended behavior)
async_task_isolation.explicit_isolation: contextvars.copy_context() can create clean copy for background tasks that must not see foreground user

memory_risk_model.leak_scenario: If set_current_user() is called but reset_current_user() is NOT called in finally block → contextvar retains reference → potential GC retention
memory_risk_model.isolation_breach: If context is not properly isolated between requests → user A could see user B's data
memory_risk_model.task_contamination: If asyncio.create_task() inherits polluted context → background task operates as wrong user

current_implementation_safety.has_try_finally: auth_middleware.py uses token pattern with finally block
current_implementation_safety.safe: Current implementation correctly uses token reset pattern

DT-002_persistence_engine.mechanism: async SQLAlchemy engine with init/shutdown lifecycle
key_functions: init_engine(backend: str, url: str, ...) — creates engine, session_factory, auto-creates tables
get_session_factory() -> async_sessionmaker | None — returns None if memory

startup_write_sequence.step_3_sqlite_setup: if backend == 'sqlite': create directory, create engine, attach WAL listeners
startup_write_sequence.step_4_postgres_setup: else if backend == 'postgres': create engine with pool_size
startup_write_sequence.step_8_postgres_retry: if postgres + 'does not exist': auto-create DB, retry create_all

memory_backend_contract.repositories_must_check: RunRepository, FeedbackRepository must check for None and fall back

memory_risk_model.connection_exhaustion: Pool size insufficient under high concurrency → 503
memory_risk_model.startup_failure: CREATE DATABASE fails → gateway fails to start
memory_risk_model.schema_drift: Base.metadata.create_all vs alembic migration → schema inconsistency
memory_risk_model.sqlite_wal_corruption: WAL file not properly checkpointed → DB corruption
memory_risk_model.engine_leak: close_engine not called on shutdown → connection leak

risks.name: ContextVar memory leak
risks.description: set_current_user called but token not reset → ContextVar holds reference to user object → GC may not collect
risks.detection: Memory profiler with tracked object counts
risks.mitigation: Always use token pattern with finally block

risks.name: Cross-request user contamination
risks.description: ContextVar not properly isolated between asyncio tasks → user A sees user B's data
risks.detection: ContextVar isolation test with concurrent requests
risks.mitigation: Each FastAPI request runs in own task; ContextVar is task-local by design

risks.name: Background task context pollution
risks.description: asyncio.create_task() inherits parent context → background task operates as wrong user
risks.detection: Unit test with explicit copy_context check
risks.mitigation: Use contextvars.copy_context() for background tasks that should not see foreground user

risks.name: Persistence engine connection exhaustion
risks.description: Connection pool size insufficient → new requests block/wait/timeout
risks.mitigation: pool_size tuning, pool_pre_ping=True, proper close_engine on shutdown

risks.name: Startup DB write failure
risks.description: CREATE DATABASE or create_all fails → gateway fails to start
risks.mitigation: memory fallback, try/except with graceful degradation

risks.name: SQLite WAL corruption
risks.description: WAL file not properly checkpointed → DB enters recovery state

contextvar_isolation_test_design.objective: Verify that per-request user context is properly isolated between concurrent asyncio tasks

test_cases.name: Sequential requests — context preserved within request
test_cases.description: Set user A, verify user A visible, reset, verify cleared
test_cases.expected: set_current_user → get_current_user returns user A → reset_current_user → get_current_user returns None

test_cases.name: Concurrent tasks — no cross-contamination
test_cases.description: Task 1 sets user A, Task 2 sets user B, both read their own user
test_cases.expected: Task1 sees user A, Task2 sees user B, neither sees other's user

test_cases.name: Background task inheritance
test_cases.description: Parent sets user A, creates child task, child sees user A (inherited)
test_cases.expected: Child task sees user A from parent context
test_cases.note: This is INTENDED behavior per asyncio spec

test_cases.name: Background task explicit isolation
test_cases.description: Parent sets user A, creates child with copy_context(), child sees None
test_cases.expected: Child task does NOT see user A (copy_context creates clean slate)
test_cases.note: Code that needs isolation must explicitly use copy_context()

test_cases.name: Token reset restores previous state
test_cases.description: Set user A, get token, set user B, reset with token A's token, verify user A
test_cases.expected: After reset, get_current_user returns user A

test_cases.name: require_current_user raises when unset
test_cases.description: Without setting any user, call require_current_user()
test_cases.expected: RuntimeError with message 'repository accessed without user context'

test_cases.name: resolve_user_id with AUTO sentinel
test_cases.description: Set user A, call resolve_user_id(AUTO) — returns str(user.id)

test_cases.name: resolve_user_id with explicit str
test_cases.description: Set user A, call resolve_user_id('explicit-id') — returns 'explicit-id'

test_cases.name: resolve_user_id with None
test_cases.description: Set user A, call resolve_user_id(None) — returns None, no user filter
test_cases.expected: Returns None (bypasses user isolation for migrations)

test_cases.name: DEFAULT_USER_ID fallback
test_cases.description: Without setting any user, call get_effective_user_id()

contextvar_isolation_test_design.pass_criteria: All 10 test cases pass with no cross-contamination detected

memory_leak_test_design.test_name: Memory Leak Test Under Sustained Load
memory_leak_test_design.objective: Verify no memory growth over 10,000 sustained requests with user context churn

test_cases.name: 10k sequential set/reset cycles
test_cases.metric: Memory before vs after, delta should be < 5MB

test_cases.name: 10k users without reset (leak detection)
test_cases.description: Loop 10k times: set_current_user(different_user) — intentionally NOT resetting
test_cases.metric: Memory growth rate — if growing linearly, leak exists

test_cases.name: Concurrent user contexts
test_cases.description: 100 concurrent tasks, each setting different user, working for 30 seconds

test_cases.name: require_current_user error path
test_cases.description: Call require_current_user() without context, verify RuntimeError, check no memory leak

memory_leak_test_design.pass_criteria: No memory leak detected, all deltas < threshold

connection_pool_stability_test_design.objective: Verify pool does not exhaust under 100 concurrent requests

test_cases.name: 50 concurrent DB reads
test_cases.description: 50 concurrent tasks, each does session.execute(SELECT), pool_size=5
test_cases.metric: All complete, no timeout, no exhaustion

test_cases.name: 100 concurrent DB operations
test_cases.description: 100 concurrent tasks, each does session.execute + commit
test_cases.metric: Pool queue, wait time, success rate

test_cases.name: Pool exhaustion recovery
test_cases.description: Deliberately exhaust pool, verify graceful rejection (not hang)

test_cases.name: Pool recycle on error
test_cases.description: Force connection error, verify pool recycles properly

test_cases.name: close_engine releases all
test_cases.description: After pool load test, call close_engine(), verify connections released

connection_pool_stability_test_design.pass_criteria: Pool exhaustion < 1% of requests, all recover gracefully

startup_db_write_safety_gate.description: Verify persistence engine startup writes are safe and reversible

checks.name: memory backend no-op
checks.description: backend=memory → init_engine returns immediately, get_session_factory returns None
checks.verification: Assert engine=None, factory=None, no DB operations

checks.name: sqlite directory creation
checks.description: sqlite backend → sqlite_dir created if not exists

checks.name: postgres database auto-create
checks.description: postgres backend with non-existent DB → CREATE DATABASE runs
checks.verification: DB exists after init_engine, create_all succeeds

checks.name: schema create_all runs
checks.description: Base.metadata.create_all executes on all registered models
checks.verification: All tables exist (users, threads_meta, runs, run_events, feedback)

checks.name: close_engine cleanup
checks.description: After init, close_engine() disposes engine, releases connections

checks.name: graceful degradation on failure
checks.description: If create_all fails, error is logged but not catastrophic
checks.verification: Engine still returns usable factory (or None if memory)

startup_db_write_safety_gate.precondition: Database backup verified before any write

database_backend_memory_fallback_contrac.description: When database.backend=memory, persistence layer must gracefully degrade

engine_behavior.init_engine: Returns immediately, logs 'Persistence backend=memory -- ORM engine not initialized'

repository_behavior.RunRepository: Must check session_factory is not None, fall back to in-memory if None
repository_behavior.FeedbackRepository: Must check session_factory is not None, fall back to no-op if None
repository_behavior.ThreadMetaStore: Must check session_factory is not None, fall back to in-memory if None

gateway_behavior._ensure_admin_user: Skips if provider not ready (catches RuntimeError)

test_contract: Repositories correctly check for None and fall back

rollback_disable_switch_design.description: Config-based switch to disable persistence and revert to memory-only mode
rollback_procedure: 4. Repositories fall back to memory implementations
5. No data loss — SQLite/Postgres data still on disk
recovery_procedure: 1. Fix root cause (memory leak, pool exhaustion, etc.)
4. init_engine recreates engine, repos use disk-backed stores
rollback_disable_switch_design.safety: memory fallback is always available — no permanent data loss path

surface010_unblock_checklist.name: Connection pool stability test (POOL-01 through POOL-05)
Startup DB write safety gate (SDB-01 through SDB-09)
memory backend fallback contract test
surface010_unblock_checklist.pass_criteria: memory mode returns None for factory/engine

gate_2.name: Memory Leak Gate
gate_2.pass_threshold: MEM-LEAK-01 through MEM-LEAK-04 all pass, delta < 5MB

gate_5.name: Rollback Disable Switch Gate
gate_5.can_proceed_if: NOT_PASSED — warning only, memory fallback available

final_gate.name: SURFACE-010 Unblock Gate
final_gate.pass_threshold: All GATE-CV-ISO + GATE-MEM-LEAK + GATE-POOL + GATE-SDB passed

layer_3_engine.reason: UserRow ORM now safe to use with session_factory

layer_5_entry.reason: init_engine safe to call at startup

layer_8_routes.reason: SURFACE-010 unblocked, GSIC-003 + GSIC-004 still block

R241-22B.reason: auth_middleware is now safe — user_context memory verified
